title: Add structured tool-contract playground and inline snippet runner
preview:
Context and motivation:
This PR introduces a compact, interactive playground in the docs site for authoring and validating structured tool contracts used by RAG agents. Our goal is to lower the onboarding time for integrators and to make it trivial to iterate on tool schemas and example snippets while seeing live te...
text:
Context and motivation:
This PR introduces a compact, interactive playground in the docs site for authoring and validating structured tool contracts used by RAG agents. Our goal is to lower the onboarding time for integrators and to make it trivial to iterate on tool schemas and example snippets while seeing live telemetry for invocation patterns. The playground targets: engineers prototyping tool contracts, solutions engineers demonstrating integrations, and docs authors writing reproducible examples.

What this change contains (single-string summary):
- New docs page at /playgrounds/structured-tool-contract with an embedded snippet runner that executes sample contracts in a sandboxed iframe and shows structured validation feedback.
- Snippet runner JS (site/static/js/snippet-runner.js) implements a short-lived local shim that validates contract JSON and emits telemetry probes to tel...

title: Multi-locale RAG quickstart and i18n evaluation playbook

preview: Motivation: customers asked for smaller, repeatable authoring artifacts that capture stable checkpoints for RAG authoring workflows and enable telemetry-driven regressions during prompt/index changes. This PR adds a compact 'checkpoint' template format, new telemetry-guided assertion examples, and an embedded author...
text: Motivation: customers asked for smaller, repeatable authoring artifacts that capture stable checkpoints for RAG authoring workflows and enable telemetry-driven regressions during prompt/index changes. This PR adds a compact 'checkpoint' template format, new telemetry-guided assertion examples, and an embedded authoring devcontainer/watch-mode recipe so engineers can iterate locally against seeded indexes. What this includes: 1) new compact checkpoint schema and examples (YAML+JSON) that capture prompt seeds, index snapshot pointers, and golden-response assertions; 2) a telemetry-guided assertions guide that maps telemetry signals (token-latency, rerank-quality, recall-window) to test thresholds and suggested remediation; 3) two new runnable samples: typescript/mini-agent and python/authoring-worker that demonstrate checkpoint-driven test runs and automatic rollback hooks; 4) a minimal...

title: Add RAG synthetic corpus authoring lab and docs
preview: Motivation: teams building Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) workflows often lack reproducible, small-scale corpora and a compact authoring loop to iterate on indexing/chunking/labeling. This PR introduces a focused "authoring lab" in the docs site: a guided walkthrough that teaches how to (1) synthesize represen...
text:
Motivation: teams building Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) workflows often lack reproducible, small-scale corpora and a compact authoring loop to iterate on indexing/chunking/labeling. This PR introduces a focused "authoring lab" in the docs site: a guided walkthrough that teaches how to (1) synthesize representative corpora from heterogeneous sources, (2) apply chunking and metadata strategies, (3) run local smoke-evals against a typed prompt set, and (4) surface telemetry to detect dataset drift. The authoring lab contains conceptual guidance, a step-by-step tutorial, runnable examples (Python and TypeScript), and a tiny browser-hosted runner for manual experimentation.

What changed (high level): added a new docs page at /docs/rag/authoring/synthetic-corpora-lab with a compact tutorial, two language examples under docs/rag/authoring/examples, a portable JS runner in site/stati...

title: Document Optimize quality gates, results interpretation, and override workflow
preview: This PR adds first-pass internal/external documentation for Optimize “quality guardrails gating” to ensure cost-saving configuration recommendations (batching, caching, quantization profiles) cannot be applied to production traffic without passing quality evaluation gates (or a time-bound, auditable override). The d...
text: This PR adds first-pass internal/external documentation for Optimize “quality guardrails gating” to ensure cost-saving configuration recommendations (batching, caching, quantization profiles) cannot be applied to production traffic without passing quality evaluation gates (or a time-bound, auditable override). The docs target both self-serve users (what you’ll see in Console, how to interpret pass/fail) and enterprise/security reviewers (audit trail, RBAC, override controls). Sections included: (1) Overview: what quality gates are and what changes they apply to (batching policy updates, prefix/KV caching settings, quantization profile switches). (2) Gate lifecycle: Proposed → Gates pending/running → Passed/Failed → Apply allowed/blocked → Rollout canary coupling. (3) Results interpretation: metrics shown, baseline selection, how to read deltas, common failure reasons (sample size, fla...

text:
## Summary
Adds the initial help-center / docs-site page for **In-console Usage Insights (Beta)**. The doc explains how the route+model breakdown is computed, defines the key metrics (tokens in/out, cache, retries/fallbacks, streaming overhead), and includes a troubleshooting section based on early beta/support feedback. This is intended to ship with the Console beta rollout and will be linked from the Usage Insights pages.

## What’s included
- New doc page: **Console → Usage → Usage Insights (Beta)**
- Definitions and attribution semantics (aligned with platform ADR/API contract)
- Guidance on interpreting cost drivers and “What changed?” deltas
- Export (CSV) overview + permissions/audit expectations
- Troubleshooting / known issues (billing reconciliation, missing route tags, time range timeouts)
- Screenshot placeholders with callouts (to be replaced once final UI polish lands)

title: Docs: audit logging event types + export destinations (SIEM/S3) with config examples
preview: Motivation: Sales/SEs have been repeatedly asked for a crisp, copy/paste-ready explanation of Redwood audit logging (what events exist, what fields are included, how to export, and what limitations/retention knobs exist by deployment mode). The existing docs were too high-level and didn’t include enough concrete con...
text:
Motivation: Sales/SEs have been repeatedly asked for a crisp, copy/paste-ready explanation of Redwood audit logging (what events exist, what fields are included, how to export, and what limitations/retention knobs exist by deployment mode). The existing docs were too high-level and didn’t include enough concrete configuration examples, which created drift between RFP language and what customers can actually implement.

What’s in this PR: (1) A new “Audit log event taxonomy” section with stable event names and a minimal field contract (so customers can build detections/ingestions without relying on internal-only fields). (2) An “Export destinations” section covering supported patterns used in the field today: pull via API, push/ship to customer-owned S3-compatible object storage, and SIEM ingestion via customer-managed forwarder (Splunk/Elastic/etc.). (3) Practical configuration exampl...

title: Document tiered SLOs + error budget policy (Hosted vs Dedicated) and escalation expectations
preview: Summary: This PR updates internal and external documentation to reflect Redwood’s tiered reliability commitments for Hosted (shared) vs Dedicated (reserved capacity) and the associated error budget policy. It explains (a) canonical SLIs and SLO targets at a high level, (b) how to interpret error budget status in Con...
text: Summary: This PR updates internal and external documentation to reflect Redwood’s tiered reliability commitments for Hosted (shared) vs Dedicated (reserved capacity) and the associated error budget policy. It explains (a) canonical SLIs and SLO targets at a high level, (b) how to interpret error budget status in Console and what it means operationally, and (c) escalation expectations and what data is required for support/SRE triage. Motivation: We’ve had repeated customer escalations around latency spikes and 5xx/stream disconnects during burst traffic, plus confusion about what counts against SLOs (client aborts, invalid requests, rate-limits, fallback/retries). This PR consolidates and standardizes wording across docs site pages used by CS/SE and customers. Scope: Hosted + Dedicated only; Private is mentioned as future work and explicitly out-of-scope for enforcement in this phase. ...

Scope: navigation + internal doc pages only; no customer-sensitive details, no new policy content (policy source of truth remains Confluence). Notes: Links use the existing internal Confluence link helper so they resolve correctly in preview/prod; link targets intentionally point at the canoni...

text:
## Summary
Adds a dedicated streaming troubleshooting section to the SDK docs and links it consistently to the new Known Issues hub articles. The goal is to reduce Support back-and-forth by giving customers copy/paste-ready guidance for the most common streaming failure modes (timeouts, reconnect, truncated streams) and by standardizing our language around safe retries and idempotency.

This PR is part of the Customer Success & Support escalation playbook refresh / known-issues sprint work. It is intentionally customer-safe (no account names, no internal dashboards).

## What’s included
- New docs page: “Troubleshooting streaming” with:
 - Common symptoms + likely causes (proxy idle timeouts, client read timeouts, network path resets)
 - Recommended client timeout defaults (connect vs read) and keepalive guidance
 - Reconnect flow for SSE/streaming responses (retry with jittered backo...

title: Internal docs: Cost Model API usage, field semantics, and troubleshooting
preview: This PR updates the internal docs site to reflect Cost Model API v1 as the canonical source for cost primitives used by Console and Optimize. It adds concrete request/response examples, defines field semantics (esp. prefill vs decode, cache attribution, batch efficiency, and overhead allocation), and includes troubl...
text:
This PR updates the internal docs site to reflect Cost Model API v1 as the canonical source for cost primitives used by Console and Optimize. It adds concrete request/response examples, defines field semantics (esp. prefill vs decode, cache attribution, batch efficiency, and overhead allocation), and includes troubleshooting guidance pulled from early integrations and support escalations.

Motivation
- We’ve had multiple definitions of “cost” across telemetry, billing hooks, Console, and Optimize prototypes. Teams need a single, versioned set of docs that matches the schema package + service behavior.
- Customer-facing escalations (SUP-31844, SUP-31877) showed that missing/ambiguous semantics (overhead vs request-attributable; optimistic what-if assumptions) cause confusion and mis-triage.

What’s included
- New internal docs page: “Cost Model API (v1) – Usage & Semantics”
 - Endpoint...

title: Docs: Shadow Traffic Quality Metrics quickstart (enablement, interpretation, retention warnings)
preview:
Title: Shadow Traffic Quality Metrics (Beta) — Quickstart

What you’ll do in this guide
In this quickstart you’ll enable Shadow Traffic on a production route and compare “control” vs “shadow” variants using quality metrics—without changing what your users see. Shadow Traffic mirrors a sampled subset of real requests...
text:
Title: Shadow Traffic Quality Metrics (Beta) — Quickstart

What you’ll do in this guide
In this quickstart you’ll enable Shadow Traffic on a production route and compare “control” vs “shadow” variants using quality metrics—without changing what your users see. Shadow Traffic mirrors a sampled subset of real requests to one or more candidate variants (e.g., new model version, quantization profile, routing policy, or prompt template), scores the shadow outputs asynchronously, and surfaces quality deltas alongside latency/cost/error metrics.

Key properties (read this first)
- No user impact: The user always receives the control response. Shadowing is fail-open: if the shadow path fails, the control path continues unaffected.
- Asynchronous quality scoring: Quality metrics may lag behind request volume (ingestion + scoring queue). Plan for delayed dashboards.
- Beta behavior: Quality sig...

title: Publish marketplace security overview + buyer FAQ (AWS/GCP)
preview: Goal: publish marketplace-friendly, customer-facing security docs that AWS/GCP reviewers and enterprise buyers can use without opening a ticket, while keeping claims accurate and consistent with our SOC 2/ISO-aligned controls. This PR adds (1) a Marketplace Security Overview page with a simple system/data-flow narra...
text:
Goal: publish marketplace-friendly, customer-facing security docs that AWS/GCP reviewers and enterprise buyers can use without opening a ticket, while keeping claims accurate and consistent with our SOC 2/ISO-aligned controls. This PR adds (1) a Marketplace Security Overview page with a simple system/data-flow narrative and clear shared responsibility boundaries, and (2) a buyer-focused FAQ that answers common procurement/security questions (encryption, audit logs, retention, residency, incident response, support SLAs, and how to request evidence).

Key points / decisions (per Security & GRC review): we avoid absolute language like "we never store prompts" and instead document our default handling + configurable controls; we call out that some security evidence (SOC 2 Type II report, pen test summary) is available under NDA / upon request; we do not claim certifications we do not hold...

title: Regional failover operator guide: automation behavior, runbook entry points, and pitfalls
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a new operator-facing guide for Redwood’s regional failover automation work (Reliability Program). The goal is to make the system understandable and operable for on-call: what the automation will do, what it will not do, where to start during an event, and how to validate outcomes.

The guide...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a new operator-facing guide for Redwood’s regional failover automation work (Reliability Program). The goal is to make the system understandable and operable for on-call: what the automation will do, what it will not do, where to start during an event, and how to validate outcomes.

The guide is intentionally written as an “operator manual” rather than a design doc: it prioritizes decision points, runbook entry points, and fast checks over implementation details.

## Why
During rehearsals and Game Day 1 we saw consistent confusion around:
- Which signals are authoritative vs corroborating (reachability vs 5xx burn vs tail latency vs capacity)
- When the controller is in dry-run vs execute mode and how to interpret recommendations
- Rollback and hold-down semantics (avoiding oscillation) and what on-call can safely override
- Where to look for the audit trail / ...

title: Add AWS/GCP Marketplace onboarding pages + entitlement troubleshooting
preview: Summary: This PR adds first-class AWS and GCP Marketplace onboarding docs to reduce time-to-first-token for marketplace buyers and to unblock partner submission requirements. It introduces a Marketplace onboarding landing page, provider-specific step-by-step guides (Subscribe → Entitlement → Provision org → Create A...
text:
Summary: This PR adds first-class AWS and GCP Marketplace onboarding docs to reduce time-to-first-token for marketplace buyers and to unblock partner submission requirements. It introduces a Marketplace onboarding landing page, provider-specific step-by-step guides (Subscribe → Entitlement → Provision org → Create API key → First request), and an entitlement troubleshooting page with concrete error states and recommended next actions. It also links to the Marketplace Security Documentation Pack and clarifies which security artifacts are public vs available on request.

Motivation / context: Marketplace buyers are procurement-led and frequently get stuck during entitlement propagation and org linking. Partner feedback requested clearer customer-facing steps and explicit guidance for common failure modes (e.g., existing orgs, multi-subscription, delayed entitlement sync). This doc set i...

title: Docs: Safe apply & rollback for Optimization Configs (Hosted/Dedicated)
preview: Summary: Adds a customer/admin-facing guide for versioned Optimization Configs, safe apply (validate → canary → promote), and one-click rollback for Hosted and Dedicated. Includes UX screenshots callouts (alt text only), API examples, and operational caveats (partial rollouts, cooldowns, metrics gating, and what rol...
text: Summary: Adds a customer/admin-facing guide for versioned Optimization Configs, safe apply (validate → canary → promote), and one-click rollback for Hosted and Dedicated. Includes UX screenshots callouts (alt text only), API examples, and operational caveats (partial rollouts, cooldowns, metrics gating, and what rollback guarantees do/don’t mean). Motivation: Repeated customer asks for reversible optimization changes (batching/caching/quant profiles) and clear change-management/audit story (refs: SUP-18467, SUP-18421). This doc is intended to be linked from Console tooltips and the Optimize docs nav. Contents: 1) Concepts: config versions, scopes (tenant/model/region), active vs promoted version, and “known-good” baseline. 2) Safe apply workflow: validate, staged rollout with canary %, gating on SLOs (5xx + latency) and quantization quality signals, pause/resume semantics. 3) Rollback...

title: Add Enterprise Security overview page (public) + SOC 2 request link stubs
preview: Context: Enterprise deals are increasingly gated on security/compliance review. We need a public, linkable Enterprise Security overview page that mirrors approved internal FAQ wording without leaking restricted evidence or promising roadmap items. This PR adds a new docs page with deployment-mode-specific notes (Hos...
text: Context: Enterprise deals are increasingly gated on security/compliance review. We need a public, linkable Enterprise Security overview page that mirrors approved internal FAQ wording without leaking restricted evidence or promising roadmap items. This PR adds a new docs page with deployment-mode-specific notes (Hosted / Dedicated / Private), clarifies our customer data handling language, and includes stubs for requesting gated artifacts (e.g., SOC 2 Type II report) via the evidence request process. Scope/Non-goals: This is documentation-only and should not imply new product functionality; any features described must already exist. The page intentionally avoids: (a) claiming ISO 27001 certification, (b) stating absolute data residency guarantees without mode/region caveats, (c) offering pen-test reports publicly, and (d) describing future roadmap for audit logging/SCIM beyond current ...

preview:

This PR adds a Private docs FAQ and a dedicated error code reference for offline bundle signature verification failures. The goal is to reduce time-to-triage for air-gapped installs by making installer failures actionable: what happened, why it happens, what to do next, and what to collect for Support.

What...
text:
Summary
This PR adds a Private docs FAQ and a dedicated error code reference for offline bundle signature verification failures. The goal is to reduce time-to-triage for air-gapped installs by making installer failures actionable: what happened, why it happens, what to do next, and what to collect for Support.

What’s new
1) FAQ: Signed offline bundles
- What is being signed?
- Can we verify without installing?
- What does "fail closed" mean?
- How do key rotations work in air-gapped environments?
- What time sources are required?
- Can we use our own signing root? (Answer: not in initial GA; roadmap note only)

2) Error code reference: RWPB-VERIFY-###
The installer and preflight tooling surface structured error codes. This doc defines each code, likely causes, and remediation steps.

Collect this for Support (include in ticket)
- Installer version (e.g., redwood-private-installer --v...

title: Private offline bundles: key rotation + trust store update guide (air-gapped)
preview: Summary: Adds customer-facing documentation for signing key rotation and trust store updates for Redwood Private offline bundles in air-gapped environments. This explains (1) how rotations are announced and staged (overlap window with multiple trusted keys), (2) how customers update trust roots offline (via a trust ...
text: Summary: Adds customer-facing documentation for signing key rotation and trust store updates for Redwood Private offline bundles in air-gapped environments. This explains (1) how rotations are announced and staged (overlap window with multiple trusted keys), (2) how customers update trust roots offline (via a trust store update package or manual key file update, depending on deployment), and (3) what Support expects when troubleshooting unknown-key and post-rotation verification failures. Motivation: Pilot customers asked for a single canonical reference that answers “what changes on rotation?”, “what do I need to copy into the air-gap?”, and “how do I prove my installer trusts the new key before attempting an upgrade?”. This PR aligns language with installer behavior (fail-closed on unknown key; break-glass requires explicit policy gating and produces audit markers) and with Release ...

title: Private: Air-gapped installation guide for signed offline bundles

text:
Motivation: Air-gapped/private customers need a single, end-to-end guide that explains (1) what a signed offline bundle is, (2) how to verify provenance/integrity offline, (3) how installation differs from connected installs, and (4) what to do when verification fails (incl. time skew and key rotation). This PR adds a full guide targeted at Redwood Private operators, plus a concise troubleshooting section and references for Support/SE.

Scope: Documentation only (no product behavior changes). The guide aligns with the installer changes landing in redwood-private-installer (inspect + preflight verify + trust store + break-glass) and the release pipeline guidance from Release Eng.

What’s included:
- New doc: "Air-gapped installation (signed offline bundles)" with prerequisites, bundle transport, verification, install flow, and upgrade notes.

text:
Summary
Adds an Ops Guide section documenting the required incident taxonomy fields (incident_type / incident_subtype / resolved_owner), how to choose a classification during a live incident, and how Customer Success / Support should interpret taxonomy-driven dashboards.

Context
As part of the Reliability Program (“Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping”), we’re making incident_type a required field in incident-bot and piping it through dashboards/alerts. This PR documents the conventions so on-call classification is consistent and reporting is usable.

What’s included
- New Ops Guide page: “Incident taxonomy fields (required)”.
- A short decision flow for classification (optimize for on-call speed + low ambiguity).
- Guidance on multi-cause incidents: primary incident_type vs contributing systems.
- How the owner is resolved (incident-bot → control plane mapping), and what to do when...

title: Document 429 reason codes, headers, and retry guidance for hot-route capacity protection
preview:
## Summary
This PR updates Redwood docs to reflect the new tier-aware rate limiting / quota enforcement and overload protection behavior being rolled out for hot enterprise routes. It documents:
- The standardized 429 error payload shape used across Hosted API endpoints
- The set of 429 reason codes and how to inter...
text:
## Summary
This PR updates Redwood docs to reflect the new tier-aware rate limiting / quota enforcement and overload protection behavior being rolled out for hot enterprise routes. It documents:
- The standardized 429 error payload shape used across Hosted API endpoints
- The set of 429 reason codes and how to interpret them (quota vs rate limit vs overload shedding)
- Response headers clients should read (including Retry-After) and recommended retry/backoff strategies
- How enterprise priority works under fleet pressure (what is and isn’t guaranteed)

This is intended to reduce customer confusion during rollout and give Support consistent language to reference.

## Why
We’ve historically overloaded multiple concepts into “429 Too Many Requests” with inconsistent messages across routes and SDKs. With hot-route capacity protection enabled, customers will see more predictable throttling...

title: Internal: add Sales Demo Guide v1 (runbook links + backup mode)
preview: Context: We’re standardizing the Redwood sales demo environment (demo tenant) and need one canonical, internal guide that SEs/AEs can follow without tribal knowledge. This PR adds an internal “Sales Demo Guide (v1)” with a 30-min run-of-show, prep checklist, tenant reset/healthcheck steps, and a “backup mode” sectio...
text: Context: We’re standardizing the Redwood sales demo environment (demo tenant) and need one canonical, internal guide that SEs/AEs can follow without tribal knowledge. This PR adds an internal “Sales Demo Guide (v1)” with a 30-min run-of-show, prep checklist, tenant reset/healthcheck steps, and a “backup mode” section for when live steps fail (quota/model/dashboard/auth issues). The guide links out to the Confluence runbooks and the demo deck/talk track, and is written to be resilient to product UI drift by emphasizing stable URLs/commands and expected outcomes rather than pixel-perfect click paths. Scope: docs only (no product changes). Security note: guide is internal-only and explicitly states that demo data must remain synthetic; includes safe-sharing rules (no exporting audit logs; no copy/pasting API keys into shared docs; do not grant external access to demo org).

text:
## Summary
This PR updates customer-facing docs so the public status page is easier to interpret (what each component represents) and incident updates are more consistent (what each update state means, what info is included/excluded).

This work supports the Status Page Transparency Upgrades initiative by reducing ambiguity around component naming and setting correct expectations during active incidents.

## Why
We’ve had repeated customer feedback that the status page was “too coarse” (e.g., Embeddings failures while API looked healthy, streaming-only degradations not reflected clearly). Support also asked for a canonical reference they can link in tickets when customers dispute accuracy.

Key goals for the copy:
- Explain component boundaries in plain language (without leaking internal service topology)
- Define incident update phases + typical contents
- Avoid over-promising SLAs o...

text:
Summary: Adds customer/operator-facing documentation for key rotation in Redwood Private/Dedicated contexts, plus an audit-friendly evidence checklist that maps cleanly to common SOC2/ISO narratives. This PR is intended to reduce back-and-forth during enterprise security reviews by making our rotation steps and evidence artifacts explicit and repeatable.

Context / why: Regulated customers (and prospects) are asking for (a) a clear rotation procedure (including rollback criteria) and (b) the exact evidence they can expect to produce/receive (who approved, what changed, when it happened, what logs prove it). This doc set aligns with the ongoing KMS hardening effort and the rotation strategy in ADR-008.

What’s included: 1) New page: “Key rotation procedure (KMS envelope encryption)” covering prerequisites, roles/responsibilities (Customer vs Redwood), staged rollout, dual-read window, ...

text:
Context
Regulated customers evaluating Redwood Private are consistently asking for a single, operator-oriented KMS integration guide that explains (1) what Redwood encrypts and where envelope encryption happens, (2) which permissions are required and why, (3) how to validate the integration before turning on production traffic, and (4) what the expected system behavior is during KMS degradation/outage.

This PR publishes the first version of the "Private deployment: Customer KMS integration operator guide" on the docs site, incorporating feedback from Support (SUP-19341 / SUP-19377) and Sales/SE security reviews (SUP-19405). It is written to be evidence-friendly for enterprise security questionnaires and to reduce back-and-forth during onboarding.

What’s included
- New operator guide page for Private deployments covering: prerequisites, supported KMS patterns (AWS/GCP/Azure conceptua...

title: Internal ops docs: Provision-to-ready readiness states, remediation, and dashboards/runbooks
preview: Motivation: With the provision-to-ready automation rollout, we need a single, up-to-date internal operator guide that explains (a) what each readiness state means, (b) what to do when nodes are stuck or failing validations, and (c) where to look first (dashboards/logs/runbooks) without rediscovering tribal knowledge...
text:
Motivation: With the provision-to-ready automation rollout, we need a single, up-to-date internal operator guide that explains (a) what each readiness state means, (b) what to do when nodes are stuck or failing validations, and (c) where to look first (dashboards/logs/runbooks) without rediscovering tribal knowledge in Slack threads. This PR updates the internal docs to match ADR-017 readiness semantics and the current control-plane/Fleet Readiness Service behavior, and standardizes remediation guidance (drain/quarantine/reimage order) to reduce risk during on-call.

Summary of changes: Adds an operator-focused "Provision-to-ready operations" page that describes the canonical states (Allocated/Provisioning/Validating/Ready/Quarantined), the authoritative signals for transitions (agent heartbeat + validation artifacts + control-plane writes), and practical triage flows for common failu...

title: Dedicated: document autoscaling bounds + predictive mode (config, semantics, troubleshooting)
preview: Summary This PR updates customer-facing documentation for Dedicated autoscaling policy bounds and Predictive Mode. It is written to support pilot->GA rollout and reduce support load by clarifying semantics (what is enforced, what is advisory), recommended starting ranges, and what to do when customers hit max bounds...
text: Summary This PR updates customer-facing documentation for Dedicated autoscaling policy bounds and Predictive Mode. It is written to support pilot->GA rollout and reduce support load by clarifying semantics (what is enforced, what is advisory), recommended starting ranges, and what to do when customers hit max bounds or experience queueing during ramps. The new docs cover: configuring min/max replicas (and GPU equivalent guidance), cooldown behavior, Predictive Mode basics, signal arbitration (reactive overrides predictions), and troubleshooting steps with references to the new dashboard panels/metrics. Motivation Recent Dedicated escalations (SUP-28411/SUP-28437) and pilot feedback indicated confusion around (1) whether max is a hard ceiling, (2) why scale-up may stop while queue grows, (3) how Predictive Mode interacts with reactive signals and cooldowns, and (4) what customers shoul...

title: Docs: Private observability minimum pack (install, dashboards, alerts, log export examples)
preview: Context: This PR documents the v1 “Private observability minimum pack” shipping with Redwood Private (VPC and on-prem). The goal is to make installs supportable without bespoke enablement by providing (1) clear install/upgrade steps, (2) a stable dashboard catalog, (3) actionable alert descriptions with runbook link...
text:
Context: This PR documents the v1 “Private observability minimum pack” shipping with Redwood Private (VPC and on-prem). The goal is to make installs supportable without bespoke enablement by providing (1) clear install/upgrade steps, (2) a stable dashboard catalog, (3) actionable alert descriptions with runbook links, and (4) supported log export examples suitable for private environments.

What’s included: Adds a new doc set under /private/observability/minimum-pack covering prerequisites (Prometheus/Grafana/Loki optional), Helm values required for the observability-pack integration, and post-install validation steps that match installer checks. Documents the dashboard catalog (Runtime Overview, Control Plane Health, GPU/Node Saturation, Queueing/Batching, and Prometheus Health) with “what to look at” notes and ownership. Adds an alert reference section mapping each baseline alert to...

title: Update Private upgrade docs: new installer commands, troubleshooting, rollback guidance
preview: Summary: Align Private (VPC + on-prem/air-gapped) upgrade/rollback documentation with the new installer workflow: plan -> prechecks -> backup -> apply -> verify -> rollback (if needed). This updates the operator-facing steps, introduces troubleshooting playbooks for common precheck and upgrade failures, and clarifie...
text: Summary: Align Private (VPC + on-prem/air-gapped) upgrade/rollback documentation with the new installer workflow: plan -> prechecks -> backup -> apply -> verify -> rollback (if needed). This updates the operator-facing steps, introduces troubleshooting playbooks for common precheck and upgrade failures, and clarifies rollback guarantees/limitations (esp. around schema migrations and partial rollouts). Motivation: Support escalations and design partner feedback indicated the previous docs were ambiguous about (a) which checks are blocking vs warning, (b) what gets backed up and how to validate restore readiness, and (c) when rollback is safe vs when manual intervention is required. This PR is the docs companion to private-installer changes (upgrade plan generator, preflight framework, backup orchestrator, rollback state machine) and Helm ordering changes (control plane before data plan...

title: Document Dedicated Burst: behavior, limits, denial reasons, and client retry guidance
preview: Summary: This PR adds customer-facing documentation for the new Dedicated “Burst Capacity” option: what it is (borrowing temporary capacity from Redwood-managed burst pools), how limits/guardrails work, how to configure it (what’s controlled by Redwood vs what customers can tune), and what clients should do when bur...
text: Summary: This PR adds customer-facing documentation for the new Dedicated “Burst Capacity” option: what it is (borrowing temporary capacity from Redwood-managed burst pools), how limits/guardrails work, how to configure it (what’s controlled by Redwood vs what customers can tune), and what clients should do when burst is denied. Motivation: During beta we saw repeated support questions around (a) why burst was denied even when baseline looked “underused”, (b) what HTTP status / reason codes mean, and (c) how retries can accidentally amplify load or trigger double-counting concerns. This doc set aims to make behavior predictable and reduce time-to-resolution for incidents. Scope: Documented (1) baseline vs burst semantics, (2) per-tenant caps (max burst ratio, absolute cap), cooldown windows, and fleet safety caps at a high level, (3) denial reasons and what the platform does (fallback...

title: Console docs: Hosted canary rollouts GA (concepts, workflow, API, troubleshooting)
preview: Summary: This PR refreshes and GA-izes our documentation for Hosted canary rollouts in Redwood Console. It consolidates beta-era notes into a single GA narrative, adds a step-by-step UI walkthrough (wizard + rollout details view), documents the relevant API surfaces at a conceptual level (with request/response snipp...
text: Summary: This PR refreshes and GA-izes our documentation for Hosted canary rollouts in Redwood Console. It consolidates beta-era notes into a single GA narrative, adds a step-by-step UI walkthrough (wizard + rollout details view), documents the relevant API surfaces at a conceptual level (with request/response snippets), and adds troubleshooting guidance aligned with the current orchestrator + policy guardrails + auto-stop behavior. Motivation: Support and design partners repeatedly asked for a single source-of-truth doc that explains (1) what a Hosted canary rollout is, (2) how percentage splits behave, (3) what guardrails/approvals exist, (4) what happens on SLO breach / auto-pause / rollback, and (5) how to debug split mismatches and “stuck” steps. This PR is intended to be the doc companion to the Hosted canary workflow GA effort. What’s included: (1) New docs page: “Hosted canary...

title: Route experiments: add customer guide + stickiness key and metrics interpretation
preview: This PR adds the initial customer-facing documentation for Route experiments (route-level A/B/n testing) in Redwood Console Rollouts. It focuses on: (1) what route experiments are and when to use them, (2) how deterministic assignment works and how to choose/pass a stickiness key to avoid skew, (3) how to interpret ...
text: This PR adds the initial customer-facing documentation for Route experiments (route-level A/B/n testing) in Redwood Console Rollouts. It focuses on: (1) what route experiments are and when to use them, (2) how deterministic assignment works and how to choose/pass a stickiness key to avoid skew, (3) how to interpret the built-in metrics (latency/cost/error/throughput + optional quality signals) and guardrails in Console, and (4) troubleshooting common beta issues (missing attribution on streaming retries, propagation lag/stale results, skew when sticky key is low entropy). The guide is intentionally light on statistical significance claims; it documents basic deltas and guardrail thresholds only (per GA scope). It also documents headers and SDK helpers surfaced during beta so users can self-debug variant assignment. Includes updates to the navigation, terminology glossary, and a small ...

title: Incident Bot v2 operator guide + troubleshooting (deploy/config diff/saturation cards)
preview: This PR updates the operator-facing documentation for Incident Bot v2 diagnostics (GA-ready). The goal is to make the bot usable in the first 5 minutes of an incident by standardizing what each context card means, how to validate it, and what to do when it’s missing/empty due to permissions or upstream timeouts. The...
text: This PR updates the operator-facing documentation for Incident Bot v2 diagnostics (GA-ready). The goal is to make the bot usable in the first 5 minutes of an incident by standardizing what each context card means, how to validate it, and what to do when it’s missing/empty due to permissions or upstream timeouts. The guide also documents degraded-mode behavior (cached context + links) and the security model for tenant-safe config diffs (redaction + audit logging + break-glass). Scope: docs only (no product/runtime changes). Motivation: recent incident retros and the beta rotation feedback showed that responders could see the cards but weren’t sure what thresholds/links were authoritative, and we repeatedly re-taught the same troubleshooting steps in #help. This adds a single canonical “Operator guide” that Support/SRE/On-call can link during active incidents. Contents: (1) Overview of ...

title: Private: publish backup/restore guide + DR checklists + troubleshooting
preview: This PR publishes customer-consumable documentation for Redwood Private backup/restore and disaster recovery (DR), aligned with the Day-2 operations toolkit shipping in the private installer and Helm charts. The goal is to give enterprise operators a repeatable path to: (1) configure backup storage securely, (2) tak...
text:
This PR publishes customer-consumable documentation for Redwood Private backup/restore and disaster recovery (DR), aligned with the Day-2 operations toolkit shipping in the private installer and Helm charts. The goal is to give enterprise operators a repeatable path to: (1) configure backup storage securely, (2) take consistent control-plane backups, (3) perform restores into a clean or existing cluster, and (4) validate DR readiness via checklists and periodic restore drills.

What’s included (new/updated docs pages):
- Private Backup & Restore Guide (end-to-end): prerequisites, supported storage backends, encryption/key management, backup create/verify flow, retention, and operational recommendations.
- DR checklists by archetype:
 - VPC single-region restore into clean cluster (same region): DNS/TLS considerations, restore ordering, post-restore validation.
 - On-prem / air-gapped ...

title: Document retention controls + compliance reporting pack request flow (incl. FAQ updates)
preview: Summary: This PR publishes initial external documentation for the Retention controls + compliance reporting pack workstream. It adds a retention controls overview (supported data classes, tier defaults, and how to configure), documents limitations and operational semantics (TTL vs purge, change history, verification...
text:
Summary: This PR publishes initial external documentation for the Retention controls + compliance reporting pack workstream. It adds a retention controls overview (supported data classes, tier defaults, and how to configure), documents limitations and operational semantics (TTL vs purge, change history, verification), and updates the Security FAQ with customer-ready answers for security questionnaires.

Motivation: Enterprise security reviews (Dedicated/Private) are consistently asking for (a) configurable retention by data class and (b) a standardized evidence package for audit logs + retention configuration. Prior docs were a mix of internal Confluence and ad-hoc SE answers; this makes the baseline story consistent and reduces back-and-forth.

What’s included:
- New docs page: “Retention controls” covering supported data classes (request/response logs where enabled, audit logs, trac...

text:
Title: Docs: add observability hooks reference for quickstart templates

Context
We’re shipping production-minded RAG + agent quickstart templates that emit standardized OpenTelemetry signals plus Redwood-specific conventions. Support/SRE feedback has been consistent: we need a canonical reference page (1) defining the required spans/attributes/metrics/log fields, and (2) mapping those to Redwood Console dashboards so users can verify instrumentation quickly and understand what they’re looking at.

What this PR does
- Adds a new docs reference page: "Observability hooks (reference)" for Quickstart Templates.
- Documents required spans + span attributes (including what is REQUIRED vs RECOMMENDED).
- Documents required metrics (names, types, units, and allowed dimensions) and includes a “cardinality guardrails” section aligned with Observability/SRE guidance.
- Adds a “Mapping to Redwoo...

title: Docs: Data residency region pinning (Hosted) — configuration, limitations, and troubleshooting
preview: This PR adds public documentation for Hosted data residency enforcement via region pinning. It covers: (1) what region pinning guarantees, (2) how to configure allowed regions + primary region in the Console and via Admin API (where available), (3) enforcement behavior (routing + fallback constraints) and common fai...
text: This PR adds public documentation for Hosted data residency enforcement via region pinning. It covers: (1) what region pinning guarantees, (2) how to configure allowed regions + primary region in the Console and via Admin API (where available), (3) enforcement behavior (routing + fallback constraints) and common failure modes, (4) the public error contract for residency violations (HTTP status, error_code, request_id, help_url), and (5) troubleshooting steps and support-ready guidance. The content is aligned with the current gateway contract (409 on deterministic policy block) and clarifies limitations/non-goals (no automatic multi-region replication; some non-content account metadata may be processed outside the pinned region). It also includes copy for streaming vs non-streaming behavior and points users to audit log + blocked-request views in Console. Internal-only implementation d...

title: Internal docs: add Unit Economics dashboard hub + link runbooks/definitions
preview: This PR updates the internal docs site to include a single “Unit Economics Dashboard” hub page and fixes/standardizes links to the canonical Confluence sources for definitions, SLOs, and oncall procedures. The goal is to reduce ping-pong across Slack threads and PR descriptions by having one stable entry point that ...
text: This PR updates the internal docs site to include a single “Unit Economics Dashboard” hub page and fixes/standardizes links to the canonical Confluence sources for definitions, SLOs, and oncall procedures. The goal is to reduce ping-pong across Slack threads and PR descriptions by having one stable entry point that Finance/Product/Eng can bookmark. The hub also documents access control expectations (feature flag + RBAC) and explicitly calls out v1 limitations so the metric isn’t over-interpreted. Changes include: (1) new internal docs page that links to the PRD, UX spec, metric dictionary, ADR for cost attribution, and Finance methodology note; (2) links to the SLO page and the oncall runbook (investigation + backfill) plus a short “how to validate freshness/completeness” checklist; (3) updated navigation entry under Observability → Cost & Usage; (4) replaced stale/notionally-correct ...

title: Docs: RBAC v2 concepts, permission catalog, role templates, and migration notes
preview:
Summary
Adds first-pass public documentation for RBAC v2 across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments. This PR introduces: (1) a conceptual overview of RBAC v2 primitives (permissions, roles, bindings, scopes), (2) a permission catalog reference page (with naming conventions and deprecation/versioning notes), (...
text:
Summary
Adds first-pass public documentation for RBAC v2 across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments. This PR introduces: (1) a conceptual overview of RBAC v2 primitives (permissions, roles, bindings, scopes), (2) a permission catalog reference page (with naming conventions and deprecation/versioning notes), (3) built-in role templates with recommended assignments, and (4) migration notes from RBAC v1/coarse roles including dual-read behavior and troubleshooting for permission denied.

Motivation / context
Enterprise customers (Dedicated + Private) are asking for least-privilege guidance and auditability artifacts. Support/Solutions also need a stable reference for: “what does this permission do?”, “which role should I assign?”, and “why am I denied?”. The docs in this PR align to the RBAC v2 enforcement + audit decision logging work landing in platform services.

What’s include...

title: Introduce RAG authoring UX walkthrough and compact step guides
preview: Motivation: teams building retrieval-augmented workflows repeatedly ask for concise authoring guidance and a lightweight in-doc walkthrough they can fork into their apps. This PR adds a compact UX-centered walkthrough for RAG authoring, short step guides targeted at SDK users, and a small interactive tutorial embedd...
text:
Motivation: teams building retrieval-augmented workflows repeatedly ask for concise authoring guidance and a lightweight in-doc walkthrough they can fork into their apps. This PR adds a compact UX-centered walkthrough for RAG authoring, short step guides targeted at SDK users, and a small interactive tutorial embedded in the docs site. The goal is to reduce onboarding time for engineers integrating retrieval + tool-calling flows and to provide reproducible local dev steps and CI smoke checks.

What this PR contains: - a new docs section `docs/rag/ux-walkthrough.md` with a step-by-step narrative of common authoring flows (indexing, prompt scaffolding, tool binding, and retry strategies); - `docs/rag/compact-steps.md`: a 1-page quick checklist for productionizing a RAG endpoint with samples for Python/TS; - `docs/snippets/ux-templates/` tiny prompt templates plus a short JSON schema for...
